subject: english
topic_title: Punctuation: Question Mark, Apostrophe, and Exclamation Mark
learning_objectives: State the uses of the question mark
Use the apostrophe for possession and contraction
Explain the effect of the exclamation mark

sections.heading: Question Mark
sections.content: A question mark ends sentences that ask for a reply. It is not used after indirect questions, and no comma or full stop should follow it.

sections.heading: Apostrophe
sections.content: The apostrophe is used to show possession and to indicate omitted letters in contractions. The note distinguishes singular and plural possessive forms and warns against adding apostrophe-s to possessive pronouns.

sections.heading: Exclamation Mark
sections.content: The exclamation mark indicates emphasis, surprise, urgency, or strong emotion. It may follow a word, phrase, or complete statement.
